title: What is the Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ)?
description: What is TDZ in JavaScript?
options: The period between entering a scope and the let/const declaration being executed, during which accessing the variable throws ReferenceError; var does not have TDZ due to hoisting
correct_answer: The period between entering a scope and the let/const declaration being executed, during which accessing the variable throws ReferenceError; var does not have TDZ due to hoisting

title: What is the difference between for...in and for...of?
description: When should you use for...in vs for...of?
options: for...in iterates over enumerable property keys (strings), suitable for objects; for...of iterates over iterable values (arrays, maps, sets, strings)
correct_answer: for...in iterates over enumerable property keys (strings), suitable for objects; for...of iterates over iterable values (arrays, maps, sets, strings)

title: What is a JavaScript Proxy object?
description: How does the Proxy object work?
options: Creates a proxy around a target object with handler traps (get, set, has, deleteProperty, apply) that intercept fundamental operations. Used to implement reactive systems, validation, logging
correct_answer: Creates a proxy around a target object with handler traps (get, set, has, deleteProperty, apply) that intercept fundamental operations. Used to implement reactive systems, validation, logging

title: What is the Reflect API in JavaScript?
description: What does Reflect do?
options: Reflect provides static methods for interceptable operations matching Proxy traps; e.g., Reflect.get(), Reflect.set(), Reflect.has(). It gives a clean way to forward operations from Proxy traps
correct_answer: Reflect provides static methods for interceptable operations matching Proxy traps; e.g., Reflect.get(), Reflect.set(), Reflect.has(). It gives a clean way to forward operations from Proxy traps

title: What are JavaScript generators and yield?
description: How do generators work?
options: function* creates a generator; yield pauses execution and returns a value; calling next() resumes. Generators are iterable and enable lazy evaluation, infinite sequences, and coroutine patterns
correct_answer: function* creates a generator; yield pauses execution and returns a value; calling next() resumes. Generators are iterable and enable lazy evaluation, infinite sequences, and coroutine patterns

title: What is the difference between Object.keys, values, entries?
description: Compare these Object methods.
options: Object.keys() returns own enumerable property names array; Object.values() returns values array; Object.entries() returns [key, value] pairs array
They all return the same thing
correct_answer: Object.keys() returns own enumerable property names array; Object.values() returns values array; Object.entries() returns [key, value] pairs array

title: What is structuredClone in JavaScript?
description: How does structuredClone work?
options: structuredClone() creates a deep copy of a value using the structured clone algorithm; handles circular references, Maps, Sets, Dates, ArrayBuffers unlike JSON.parse(JSON.stringify())
correct_answer: structuredClone() creates a deep copy of a value using the structured clone algorithm; handles circular references, Maps, Sets, Dates, ArrayBuffers unlike JSON.parse(JSON.stringify())

title: What is optional chaining and nullish coalescing?
description: How do ?. and ?? work together?
options: ?. safely accesses nested properties (returns undefined if null/undefined); ?? provides a default value only for null/undefined (not 0 or ''). Combined: user?.address?.city ?? 'Unknown'
correct_answer: ?. safely accesses nested properties (returns undefined if null/undefined); ?? provides a default value only for null/undefined (not 0 or ''). Combined: user?.address?.city ?? 'Unknown'

title: Implement a JavaScript Observable pattern.
description: Create a simple Observable class.

title: Implement Array.prototype.reduce from scratch.
description: Write a polyfill for Array.reduce.

title: What is the difference between microtasks and macrotasks?
description: How does the event loop prioritize microtasks and macrotasks?
options: Microtasks (Promise.then, queueMicrotask, MutationObserver) execute after the current task completes but before the next macrotask. Macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval, I/O) queue for the next event loop iteration
correct_answer: Microtasks (Promise.then, queueMicrotask, MutationObserver) execute after the current task completes but before the next macrotask. Macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval, I/O) queue for the next event loop iteration

title: What is the difference between call, apply, bind?
description: How do call, apply, and bind differ?
options: call(thisArg, arg1, arg2) invokes immediately with individual args; apply(thisArg, [args]) invokes immediately with array; bind(thisArg, arg1) returns a new function with bound context
correct_answer: call(thisArg, arg1, arg2) invokes immediately with individual args; apply(thisArg, [args]) invokes immediately with array; bind(thisArg, arg1) returns a new function with bound context

title: Implement a LazyLoader utility.
description: Create a function that lazy loads modules.

title: What are JavaScript symbols?
description: What are Symbols and their use cases?
options: Symbols are unique, immutable primitive values used as property keys to avoid name collisions; well-known symbols (Symbol.iterator, Symbol.toPrimitive) customize object behavior
correct_answer: Symbols are unique, immutable primitive values used as property keys to avoid name collisions; well-known symbols (Symbol.iterator, Symbol.toPrimitive) customize object behavior

title: Implement a retry mechanism with backoff.
description: Write a function that retries an async operation with exponential backoff.

title: What is the import.meta object?
description: What does import.meta provide?
options: import.meta is a meta-property exposing context-specific metadata about the current module; import.meta.url gives the module's URL. In Vite: import.meta.env for env variables
correct_answer: import.meta is a meta-property exposing context-specific metadata about the current module; import.meta.url gives the module's URL. In Vite: import.meta.env for env variables

title: Implement a simple state machine.
description: Create a finite state machine implementation.

title: What is the AbortController API?
description: How does AbortController work?
options: AbortController creates a signal that can abort async operations like fetch; controller.abort() triggers signal.aborted = true; pass signal to fetch options. Also works with addEventListener
correct_answer: AbortController creates a signal that can abort async operations like fetch; controller.abort() triggers signal.aborted = true; pass signal to fetch options. Also works with addEventListener

title: Implement a PubSub with wildcard subscriptions.
description: Create a publish-subscribe system supporting wildcard patterns.

title: What is the difference between WeakRef and FinalizationRegistry?
description: How do WeakRef and FinalizationRegistry work?
options: WeakRef holds a weak reference to an object (can be garbage collected); FinalizationRegistry registers a callback when an object is collected. Used for caches and resource cleanup
correct_answer: WeakRef holds a weak reference to an object (can be garbage collected); FinalizationRegistry registers a callback when an object is collected. Used for caches and resource cleanup
